description: THE FLAGSHIP DRIFT EXPERIMENT (arbitrates generics, interfaces, metaprogramming at once — all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence): same algorithm family generated as (a) generic template + explicit instantiations, (b) N copies under a substitution-equality/contract-group checker, (c) N unchecked copies, across repeated edit/regeneration cycles; measure semantic drift rate, drift ESCAPING the checker, law-annotation desynchronization, tokens generated+re-read, repair iterations, and whether writers regenerate ALL copies after a template edit.

description: Redundancy-independence (decides type-inference): mandatory interior annotations vs elided interiors + elaboration records; measure (a) rate interior annotations catch real semantic errors the elided regime compiles wrong, (b) correlated-wrongness rate (annotation and code both wrong, typechecks), (c) trivial-mismatch friction and iterations-to-green; plus token economics on read-modify-write tasks including deep generic pipelines (the O(k^2) claim) and re-read error rates raw-source vs elaborated views.

description: Defunctionalization drift (decides the closures desugaring-layer reopener): manual env-structs with mandated exactness diagnostics vs explicit-capture-list closures desugaring to identical IR; measure silent-semantic-divergence rate (snapshot-vs-live field-type choice, population-site wrong-referent wiring), fraction caught at compile time, repair-loop length per diagnostic class; pre-commit the threshold that adds the desugaring layer.

description: Dispatch-edit economics (dynamic-dispatch reopener): edit-list-guided O(K) switch-site updates after variant addition vs O(1) dyn impl edits — drift and repair success rates; arm-body divergence across K duplicate sites with/without the one-definition table rule; plus a PREVALENCE CENSUS of closed vs open dispatch sites in performance-critical generated code (the verdict's load-bearing assumption).

description: SILENT-WRONG BATTERY: seeded tasks whose natural failure is well-typed-wrong (region pessimization, context-dropping Result chains, operand swaps); spec-taught vs repair-taught writers; also tests whether redundancy annotations retain checksum independence or collapse toward checker-satisfaction.
blocks: Spec architecture hardening; numeric cap authority; surface/lexicon decision

description: REPAIR-LOOP EXPOSURE-DECAY PROBE (confound control): multi-turn regeneration measuring whether session-accumulated canonical-form text dominates penalty decay over curated spec examples — without it the surface-experiment kill thresholds are confounded.

description: SUPPORTING OBLIGATIONS: loop-viability gates (closed-world memoized check-loop pricing, line 98; multi-process trap=abort wall-clock, line 72) with pre-committed reopen thresholds; adversarial verification of D009/D010/C002 before further load-bearing use; K001 (Austral) cited into the compiler-as-teacher record; benchmark-transfer validity study before the pack CI gate graduates from advisory; RE-VALIDATION CADENCE recorded — every measured number is a model-generation snapshot, re-validated per major model generation with pre-committed reopening margins.

description: Rust-divergence audit of the full spec lexicon per D3: classify every surface keyword (Ok/Err/Some/None, box, fn, let, contract, conform, angle-bracket generics) as evidence-backed, census-passed mnemonic, or rename candidate; record per-keyword rulings under LEX-1.

description: Draft the batch-replay equivalence rule for trap semantics (permits batch-deferred stores + scalar replay in vectorized bodies; preserves precise trap identity), and validate the three-tier analysis (induction-proof elimination / masked elementwise / reduction restructure) against LLVM LoopVectorize + ScalarEvolution behavior with FileCheck tests.

description: R3 validation of the single loop form: compare bare loop+break vs counted for-range vs iterator-op forms on AI codegen metrics (first-parse success, off-by-one rate, bounds-proof friendliness per the Area-2 trap-elimination analysis, token cost). Current loop+break was selected for grammar minimality, not validated; PROVISIONAL under R3.

description: R3 sweep of kernel v0.2: for every construct, record whether the retained single form was evidence-selected (best for AI codegen + performance among candidates) or minimality-selected; minimality-selected forms are provisional and need selection rationale or experiments.

description: W1 rule: every AI-codegen experiment (drift, loop-form, spec-size ablation, syntax three-arm, pedagogy) runs across model capability tiers; pre-committed criterion: a form that only frontier-tier models use correctly fails W1 regardless of frontier-tier results.

description: Build the AI-codegen validation harness R3 presupposes: no instrument in the record measures first-parse success, rule-violation rate, repair-loop iterations, or provability outcomes for generated code (implementation/validation-harness-plan.md targets optimizer soundness only).
